events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need interpret scenario: commercial construction, fall protection, observed field condition: "6-ft trigger ignored". Work rough-complete. Need estimate measurable condition and state why out of tolerance. Reference OSHA 1926 Subpart M and 1910.140. The 6-ft trigger refers to OSHA requirement that fall protection required at heights 6 feet or more in construction (1926 Subpart M). Ignoring trigger means workers exposed at >=6 ft without protection. So decision fail, risk high/critical? Since fall from height, potential fatal. Use critical maybe high. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 80. value: 6 (feet) or maybe estimate exposure height; use 6 as trigger threshold. confidence 1.0. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty. findings: workers exposed at/above 6 ft without fall protection; missing guardrails/PPE/anchors; violation of 6-foot trigger. actions: immediately provide fall protection, stop work, install guardrails/safety nets/PFAS, train, inspect. rationale. references: OSHA 1926 Subpart M 1926.501(b)(1) etc, 1910.140. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",

  "findings": [
    "Fall protection 6-foot trigger is being ignored",
    "Workers are exposed at or above 6 ft without guardrails, safety nets, or personal fall arrest systems",
    "No compliant fall protection system is in place for the observed elevation"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately in the affected area",
    "Provide compliant fall protection before work resumes",
    "Install guardrails, safety nets, or personal fall arrest systems as required",
    "Verify anchorages and PFAS equipment meet 1910.140 and 1926 Subpart M",
    "Retrain workers on the 6-foot trigger and re-inspect"
  ],
  "rationale": "OSHA 1926 Subpart M requires fall protection when construction workers are exposed to falls of 6 feet or more. The observed condition shows the 6-ft trigger was ignored, meaning the exposed height meets or exceeds the threshold without adequate protection, creating an immediate life-safety hazard.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart M",
    "OSHA 1926.501(b)(1)",
    "OSHA 1910.140"
  ]
}
